skill.name: advanced-evaluation
skill.description: Guides teams through LLM-as-judge evaluation design, scoring rubrics, pairwise comparisons, metric selection, and bias mitigation.
skill.summary: Build reliable LLM evaluation workflows with rubrics, judge prompts, metrics, and bias checks.
skill.author: ChakshuGautam

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, network, and combined-risk alerts. Manual review found the command and cryptography alerts are documentation false positives from markdown fences, evaluation terms, examples, and citations. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious runtime behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to markdown code fences and illustrative Python snippets. These are examples for evaluation pipelines, not shell execution paths or Ruby backtick commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are fenced documentation examples or prompt templates. No code path executes shell commands from these lines.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in evaluation documentation and sample scoring data. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic API usage was found in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe evaluation concepts or criteria data, not cryptographic operations. The scanner appears to match unrelated text patterns.

low_findings.title: External URLs Are Research Citations
low_findings.description: The network alerts point to arXiv and research article links in the references section. They are static citations and do not create outbound network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are markdown links under External research. There is no code that fetches or transmits data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Packaged Demonstration Script Has Limited Local Behavior
low_findings.description: The repository includes a Python example script. The reviewed entry point prints demonstration results and does not read secrets, write files, or call remote endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script has executable behavior, but the entry point only calls local examples and prints output. Residual risk is limited to normal script-review expectations.

content.value_statement: LLM evaluation is hard when scoring, bias, and metrics are inconsistent. This skill guides reliable judge prompts, rubrics, comparisons, and validation workflows.
actual_capabilities: Select direct scoring, pairwise comparison, or reference-based evaluation for a task.
Create criteria, scoring scales, and rubrics for automated or human review.
Design pairwise comparisons with position-swap checks and confidence calibration.
Choose validation metrics such as precision, recall, F1, kappa, and rank correlation.
Identify position, length, verbosity, authority, and self-enhancement bias in judge workflows.
Plan evaluation pipelines with screening, detailed review, and human escalation.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a complete hosted evaluation service.
It does not guarantee agreement between automated judges and human reviewers.
It requires representative test data to validate any evaluation workflow.
It does not replace expert review for high-stakes or regulated decisions.

use_cases.title: Compare Model Outputs
use_cases.description: Product teams can compare candidate model responses with pairwise judging and position bias checks.

use_cases.title: Build Scoring Rubrics
use_cases.description: Engineering teams can define clear criteria, weights, and score levels for repeatable automated evaluation.

use_cases.title: Validate Judge Quality
use_cases.description: Research teams can measure agreement between automated judges and human reviewers using appropriate metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Evaluation Method
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner selection between direct scoring and pairwise comparison.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose an evaluation method for this task: [describe task]. Explain whether direct scoring, pairwise comparison, or reference-based evaluation fits best.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Rubric
prompt_templates.scenario: Define a repeatable rubric for one response quality criterion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 1-5 rubric for evaluating [criterion] in [domain]. Include level descriptions, observable characteristics, and edge case guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Design Pairwise Evaluation
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare two model outputs while reducing position bias.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a pairwise comparison workflow for responses A and B to this prompt: [prompt]. Include position swapping, tie rules, and confidence calibration.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Evaluation Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing judge workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this LLM evaluation pipeline: [pipeline details]. Identify bias risks, missing validation metrics, weak rubrics, and human review checkpoints.

output_examples.input: A team needs to score support answers for accuracy and completeness.
output_examples.output: Recommended direct scoring with separate criteria for accuracy, completeness, and clarity. Use a 1-5 rubric and validate against human reviews.

output_examples.input: Two models produce different marketing summaries for the same brief.
output_examples.output: Recommended pairwise comparison with position swapping. Judge tone, relevance, factual accuracy, and usefulness before selecting a winner.

output_examples.input: An automated judge often rewards longer answers.
output_examples.output: Add explicit conciseness criteria, monitor length-score correlation, and penalize repeated or irrelevant content in the rubric.

best_practices: Require evidence-based justification before any score or winner selection.
Swap response positions in pairwise comparisons and reduce confidence when results disagree.
Validate automated judgments against human review before using results for important decisions.
anti_patterns: Using one vague overall quality score for unrelated criteria.
Running single-pass pairwise comparisons without checking position bias.
Treating automated judge scores as ground truth without calibration data.

faq.question: What does this skill help evaluate?
faq.answer: It helps evaluate LLM outputs using rubrics, judge prompts, pairwise comparisons, metrics, and bias checks.

faq.question: Does it run an evaluation service?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, templates, and example patterns for building or reviewing evaluation workflows.

faq.question: When should I use direct scoring?
faq.answer: Use direct scoring when criteria are objective, such as factual accuracy, instruction following, or format compliance.

faq.question: When should I use pairwise comparison?
faq.answer: Use pairwise comparison when judging preferences such as tone, style, usefulness, or persuasive quality.

faq.question: How does it address bias?
faq.answer: It covers position swaps, length controls, blind evaluation, relevance weighting, and evidence-based scoring.

faq.question: Can it replace human reviewers?
faq.answer: No. It recommends human validation and escalation for low-confidence, high-impact, or regulated use cases.
